GA4 201 Micro-Application — Explorations (Deeper Analysis)

Module 201: Dive deeper into GA4 data and reports (Intermediate)

Author

Asif Kachroo

Published

February 27, 2026

1 1. Training Completion Evidence

Below are screenshots showing completion of all submodules for the GA4 201 module: Dive deeper into GA4 data and reports (Intermediate).

Training Evidence Screenshot 1

2 2. Exploration Evidence Screenshots

This analysis uses Explore → Funnel exploration in the GA4 Google Merch Shop demo property to understand the purchase journey and where users drop off.

2.1 Figure 1 — Exploration setup (steps/segments)

2.2 Figure 2 — Exploration results (drop-offs)

3 3. Short Responses

3.1 What question were you trying to answer?

I wanted to understand where users drop off in the ecommerce journey and identify the biggest barrier preventing users from completing a purchase.

3.2 What did you find?

  • Out of 14,684 users who viewed an item, only 3,534 added a product to their cart, which is a 78.1% drop-off from the first step.
  • From Add to cart → Begin checkout, there was a 51.3% drop-off (only 1,741 users reached checkout).
  • From Begin checkout → Purchase, there was a 51.2% drop-off (only 850 users completed purchase).
  • The overall conversion rate from View Item → Purchase was 5.8% (850 out of 14,684).

3.3 What’s your next investigation step based on the results? (1–2 sentences)

Next, I would break the funnel down by device category (mobile vs desktop) more closely and compare drop-offs to see where mobile users struggle. I would also check traffic source/medium to see whether certain channels are bringing lower-intent users.

3.4 What’s one action you would recommend for your CEP/client?

  • Improve product pages to increase add-to-cart rate (stronger CTA, clearer pricing/shipping info, and better product visuals).
  • Reduce checkout friction (fewer steps/fields and improved mobile checkout experience) to lower abandonment between checkout and purchase.
